conjecture_title: Informal conjecture on improved color ratio in Theorem 5
paper_title: Fractional coloring of planar graphs of girth five
summary: No follow-up work addressing this specific informal conjecture was found in the indexed literature. The conjecture proposes a modest improvement over Theorem 5 of the source paper: a coloring by subsets of {1,...,9} where every vertex receives at least 3 colors and the distinguished vertex x receives 5 colors. The source paper was published in SIAM Journal on Discrete Mathematics (2019), but subsequent work on fractional/circular coloring of planar graphs of girth five (e.g., Hu 2022, arXiv:2007.00182) does not appear to address this specific ratio improvement.

notes: No follow-up found after 4 web searches (plus 1 WebFetch on arXiv:2007.00182). The conjecture is very specific and informal, embedded in a remark following Theorem 5 of the source paper. The paper appeared in SIAM J. Discrete Math. (DOI: 10.1137/18M1214068). The conjecture is recent (2018) and the absence of any follow-up is consistent with open status at high confidence.
